items.title: I shipped a real product for $29.63 with five AI agents
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer open-sourced the exact codebase and multi-agent architecture they used to ship a real, working product on a tiny budget. Since you are building AI-ingest to help you ship, studying their lightweight orchestration setup can save you from over-engineering. Try cloning this repository this week, looking at how they structured their bootstrap prompts, and adapting one of their agent templates to generate the next feature for your AI-ingest tool.

items.title: Claude Code as a Daily Driver: Claude.md, Skills, Subagents, Plugins, and MCPs
items.why_it_matters: This post breaks down how to optimize Claude Code using custom skills, subagents, and a system-level configuration file to guide the agent. Since you rely on Claude Code to write your code, setting this up will make your 'vibe coding' sessions much faster and more accurate. Try creating a basic `claude.md` file in your AI-ingest repo this week containing your project rules and tech stack so Claude Code stops making assumptions.

items.title: Introducing Chat Mode – A modern chat interface for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: A developer built a modern chat interface wrapper specifically for Claude Code. If you find the default terminal interface of Claude Code too restrictive for reviewing and directing large blocks of code, this gives you a cleaner visual layout. Try installing this wrapper this week to see if a visual chat interface makes it easier for you to verify and direct Claude's code changes.

items.title: Show HN: AI agent token cost calculator for Codex and Claude Code loops
items.why_it_matters: This tool calculates the exact token costs of running agent loops in Codex and Claude Code. Since you use these tools daily, keeping an eye on token spend prevents surprise API bills and helps you optimize your prompts. Try pasting your recent terminal history or Claude Code logs into this calculator this week to see exactly how much your current development loop is costing you.

items.title: Show HN: Agent Launch – One CLI for Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini, OpenCode
items.why_it_matters: This lightweight CLI tool lets you launch different local coding agents (like Codex and Claude Code) using a single unified command instead of remembering different flags. Try installing `agent-launch` via npm this week and use the `agl` command to quickly spin up your preferred coding agent inside your AI-ingest directory.

items.title: A Board Game Agent Built Using Sanity Context and Vercel's AI SDK
items.why_it_matters: This project demonstrates how to build an interactive agent using Vercel's AI SDK, which fits perfectly into your Next.js and Vercel stack. Try looking at their Vercel AI SDK implementation code this week to see how they stream agent responses, and use that same pattern to stream the generation of your morning briefs in AI-ingest.
